Image enhancement experiment

SuperResolution

I built this project around a video super-resolution pipeline: source clips are split into frames, each frame can be treated as low-resolution input, then reconstructed into a sharper high-resolution preview.

For hosting, I converted the idea into a static browser demo. The page takes one extracted frame, simulates downsampling, upscales it back, and applies an edge-recovery pass on canvas so the enhancement flow is visible without running a Python model server.

Frame extraction from source video Low-resolution simulation by downsampling Canvas reconstruction with smoothing and sharpening
Drag across the image Before and after comparison
Degraded input Enhanced output
Working size 960 x 540
Low-res source 240 x 135
Recovery pass 55%
